intro: Schradera monantha, also known as the "single-flowered Schradera," is a species of plant in the Rubiaceae family, native to tropical regions of Asia and the Pacific Islands. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Schradera monantha include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Schradera monantha is commonly prepared as a decoction, tincture, or infusion, and is used to treat respiratory infections, fever, and inflammation.
